title: Nursery Rhyme. CCCLXV. Paradoxes.
content:
Up stairs, down stairs, upon my lady's window,
        There I saw a cup of sack and a race of ginger;
        Apples at the fire, and nuts to crack,
        A little boy in the cream-pot up to his neck.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Unknown, titled "Nursery Rhyme. CCCLXV. Paradoxes.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
